former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} / {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715|1=July 31}} (summer festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kamo Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Kamo Jinja Iriguchi.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamo, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, and its former rank was gōsha. === History === It enshrines the ancestral deity of the "Kamo Hōri-be" in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=UNKNOWN}} under Settsu sacred lineages. Records were lost in a fire in the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era (1781-1788), but the shrine still possesses a single white-feather arrow said to have been dedicated by Prince Shino, an imperial descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it became a village shrine. * In February of Meiji 14 (1881), promoted to gōsha. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45 (1970), shrine buildings were newly builtSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === As of 2019, the deities of precinct shrines are said to be completely unknown (hearing from the chief priest). Deity names from unclear sources are left here for reference. * Kasuga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * Amaterasu Kōdaijin Shrine Hiwakamiya-no-Mikoto * Atago Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Taga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Aragami Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Kumano Shrine {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * Inari Shrine Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Hachiman Shrine {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Matsuo Shrine Yasogami === Precinct === ファイル:Kamo Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Kamo Jinja 2Torii.jpg|Approach ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha1.jpg|Precinct shrines (right) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha2.jpg|Precinct shrines (left) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha3.jpg|Enju Shrine ファイル:Kamo Jinja Haraidokoro.jpg|Purification place === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Site (Kawanishi)|Q18701075|WD=Q18701075|lt=Kamo Site|1=Kamo site}} (National {{ill|lt=Historic Site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}, designated July 31, 2000) : Settlement remains from the {{ill|lt=Paleolithic|Q11768|WD=Q11768|1=Ancient Egypt}} and {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} through the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the Kawanishi City Cultural Properties Museum. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station|Q855900|WD=Q855900|1=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Line|Q1154538|WD=Q1154538|1=Fukuchiyama Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== * [http://www.kamo-jinja.com/ Official website of Kamo Shrine] {{shinto-stub}}{{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq old]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} / {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715|1=July 31}} (summer festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kamo Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Kamo Jinja Iriguchi.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamo, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, and its former rank was gōsha. === History === It enshrines the ancestral deity of the "Kamo Hōri-be" in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=UNKNOWN}} under Settsu sacred lineages. Records were lost in a fire in the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era (1781-1788), but the shrine still possesses a single white-feather arrow said to have been dedicated by Prince Shino, an imperial descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it became a village shrine. * In February of Meiji 14 (1881), promoted to gōsha. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45 (1970), shrine buildings were newly builtSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === As of 2019, the deities of precinct shrines are said to be completely unknown (hearing from the chief priest). Deity names from unclear sources are left here for reference. * Kasuga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * Amaterasu Kōdaijin Shrine Hiwakamiya-no-Mikoto * Atago Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Taga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Aragami Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Kumano Shrine {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * Inari Shrine Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Hachiman Shrine {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Matsuo Shrine Yasogami === Precinct === ファイル:Kamo Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Kamo Jinja 2Torii.jpg|Approach ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha1.jpg|Precinct shrines (right) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha2.jpg|Precinct shrines (left) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha3.jpg|Enju Shrine ファイル:Kamo Jinja Haraidokoro.jpg|Purification place === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Site (Kawanishi)|Q18701075|WD=Q18701075|lt=Kamo Site|1=Kamo site}} (National {{ill|lt=Historic Site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}, designated July 31, 2000) : Settlement remains from the {{ill|lt=Paleolithic|Q11768|WD=Q11768|1=Ancient Egypt}} and {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} through the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the Kawanishi City Cultural Properties Museum. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station|Q855900|WD=Q855900|1=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Line|Q1154538|WD=Q1154538|1=Fukuchiyama Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== * [http://www.kamo-jinja.com/ Official website of Kamo Shrine] {{shinto-stub}}{{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]] [[Category:Qq old]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|WD=Q2963|1=October 16}} / {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|WD=Q2715|1=July 31}} (summer festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kamo Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Kamo Jinja Iriguchi.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamo, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|WD=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|WD=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|WD=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, and its former rank was gōsha. === History === It enshrines the ancestral deity of the "Kamo Hōri-be" in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=UNKNOWN}} under Settsu sacred lineages. Records were lost in a fire in the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|WD=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era (1781-1788), but the shrine still possesses a single white-feather arrow said to have been dedicated by Prince Shino, an imperial descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|WD=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it became a village shrine. * In February of Meiji 14 (1881), promoted to gōsha. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45 (1970), shrine buildings were newly builtSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === As of 2019, the deities of precinct shrines are said to be completely unknown (hearing from the chief priest). Deity names from unclear sources are left here for reference. * Kasuga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|WD=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * Amaterasu Kōdaijin Shrine Hiwakamiya-no-Mikoto * Atago Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Taga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Aragami Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Kumano Shrine {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * Inari Shrine Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Hachiman Shrine {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Matsuo Shrine Yasogami === Precinct === ファイル:Kamo Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Kamo Jinja 2Torii.jpg|Approach ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha1.jpg|Precinct shrines (right) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha2.jpg|Precinct shrines (left) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha3.jpg|Enju Shrine ファイル:Kamo Jinja Haraidokoro.jpg|Purification place === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Site (Kawanishi)|Q18701075|WD=Q18701075|lt=Kamo Site|1=Kamo site}} (National {{ill|lt=Historic Site|Q1081138|WD=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}, designated July 31, 2000) : Settlement remains from the {{ill|lt=Paleolithic|Q11768|WD=Q11768|1=Ancient Egypt}} and {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|WD=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} through the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the Kawanishi City Cultural Properties Museum. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station|Q855900|WD=Q855900|1=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Line|Q1154538|WD=Q1154538|1=Fukuchiyama Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== * [http://www.kamo-jinja.com/ Official website of Kamo Shrine] {{shinto-stub}}{{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 16|Q2963|qid=Q2963|1=October 16}} / {{ill|lt=July 31|Q2715|qid=Q2715|1=July 31}} (summer festival) |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Kamo Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[ファイル:Kamo Jinja Iriguchi.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Precinct entrance}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamo, {{ill|lt=Kawanishi|Q609350|qid=Q609350|1=Kawanishi, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|lt=Kōbe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}, and its former rank was gōsha. === History === It enshrines the ancestral deity of the "Kamo Hōri-be" in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=UNKNOWN}} under Settsu sacred lineages. Records were lost in a fire in the {{ill|lt=Tenmei|Q1194121|qid=Q1194121|1=Tenmei}} era (1781-1788), but the shrine still possesses a single white-feather arrow said to have been dedicated by Prince Shino, an imperial descendant of {{ill|lt=Emperor Uda|Q349267|qid=Q349267|1=Emperor Uda}}. * In August of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 (1873), it became a village shrine. * In February of Meiji 14 (1881), promoted to gōsha. * In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 45 (1970), shrine buildings were newly builtSadatoshi Yoshii, "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Vol. 5" (1977) {{全国書誌番号|78005760}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|qid=Q11634943|lt=Wakeikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}} === Precinct shrines === As of 2019, the deities of precinct shrines are said to be completely unknown (hearing from the chief priest). Deity names from unclear sources are left here for reference. * Kasuga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} * Amaterasu Kōdaijin Shrine Hiwakamiya-no-Mikoto * Atago Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Taga Shrine {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Kami|1=Ōkuninushi}} * Aragami Shrine Amekagutsuchi-no-Kami * Kumano Shrine {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} * Inari Shrine Ōtsuki-hime-no-Mikoto * Hachiman Shrine {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * Matsuo Shrine Yasogami === Precinct === ファイル:Kamo Jinja Honden.jpg|Honden ファイル:Kamo Jinja 2Torii.jpg|Approach ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha1.jpg|Precinct shrines (right) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha2.jpg|Precinct shrines (left) ファイル:Kamo Jinja Keidaisha3.jpg|Enju Shrine ファイル:Kamo Jinja Haraidokoro.jpg|Purification place === Historic site === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Site (Kawanishi)|Q18701075|qid=Q18701075|lt=Kamo Site|1=Kamo site}} (National {{ill|lt=Historic Site|Q1081138|qid=Q1081138|1=Historic site}}, designated July 31, 2000) : Settlement remains from the {{ill|lt=Paleolithic|Q11768|qid=Q11768|1=Ancient Egypt}} and {{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|qid=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}} through the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. Excavated artifacts are displayed at the Kawanishi City Cultural Properties Museum. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station|Q855900|qid=Q855900|1=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Line|Q1154538|qid=Q1154538|1=Fukuchiyama Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== * [http://www.kamo-jinja.com/ Official website of Kamo Shrine] {{shinto-stub}}{{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]]

Excavated artifacts are displayed at the Kawanishi City Cultural Properties Museum. === Transportation === * About 8 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station|Q855900|qid=Q855900|1=Kawanishi-Ikeda Station}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Fukuchiyama Line|Q1154538|qid=Q1154538|1=Fukuchiyama Line}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ===External links=== * [http://www.kamo-jinja.com/ Official website of Kamo Shrine] {{shinto-stub}}{{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kamo shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kawanishi, Hyogo]] [[Category:川西市の歴史]] [[Category:Kamo Shrines]]
